description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §13.3 calls for `roster_hash` in checkpoint meta and warnings on mismatch. `StoreRuntime::checkpoint_snapshot` always sets `roster_hash = None`, and there is no roster hash computation anywhere. This loses the audit trail for durability eligibility changes.

**Design**
- Compute roster hash from validated `replicas.toml` (canonical JSON) when present.
- Include it in `CheckpointSnapshot` + meta; surface mismatch warnings on import.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] roster_hash is present when a replica roster exists.
- [ ] Checkpoint import compares roster_hash and emits a warning on mismatch.
- [ ] Tests cover hash stability + mismatch warning.

**Files:** src/daemon/store_runtime.rs, src/git/checkpoint/meta.rs, src/git/checkpoint/import.rs
title: Checkpoint meta never includes roster_hash

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] subscribe_gates_on_require_min_seen passes reliably.
- [ ] Subscribe stream failure modes return a structured error (not just disconnect) when possible.
- [ ] Test setup avoids daemon lifecycle races if needed.
description:
**Problem**
subscribe_gates_on_require_min_seen fails in tests/integration/daemon/subscribe.rs:104 with Ipc(Disconnected) during the normal integration run (cargo test --test integration). This indicates the subscription stream closes unexpectedly during require_min_seen gating. The same test passed in a subsequent slow-tests run, suggesting a race or lifecycle issue rather than a deterministic assertion failure.

**Repro**
- cargo test --test integration (failed on 2026-01-15)

**Files**
- tests/integration/daemon/subscribe.rs
- src/daemon/ipc/* (subscribe stream), src/daemon/core.rs (subscribe handling), broadcaster/gating logic
design: Investigate why the IPC subscription stream closes during require_min_seen gating. Add structured logging around subscribe open/close paths and daemon lifecycle. Verify the daemon is started and the subscribe request is not racing daemon startup or shutdown. If the stream closes due to a specific error (gate timeout, overload, unexpected payload), surface that explicitly instead of a bare disconnect. Stabilize the test by ensuring the daemon is ready before subscribing or by retrying transient disconnects if the daemon is still live.
title: Integration test failure: subscribe require_min_seen disconnects

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] No `unreachable!` or panics for Unknown heads in repl/gap buffer/executor paths.
- [ ] Types distinguish known vs unknown head states; durable/applied paths only accept known.
- [ ] Snapshot ingestion/decoding validates head presence explicitly.
- [ ] All existing tests updated; add one compile-time or conversion test to ensure Unknown cannot reach session state.
description:
**Problem**
`HeadStatus::Unknown` exists in core and can be constructed for any watermark, yet several runtime paths treat it as impossible and `unreachable!()` if it appears. This means the type admits a state the system claims cannot happen. It violates “types tell the truth” and creates latent panics if a future refactor or new codepath produces Unknown.

Key references:
- `crates/beads-core/src/watermark.rs:118` — `HeadStatus` includes `Unknown`.
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/session.rs:727` — `unreachable!("unknown head should never reach session state")`.
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/gap_buffer.rs:287` — same assumption.
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/executor.rs:840` — “durable watermark head should be known”.

Severity: latent panic in WAL/repl and mutation pipelines; correctness depends on a convention rather than an enforced invariant.
design:
**Design**
Encode “known head” vs “unknown head” in types rather than runtime branches.

Option A (split enum):
- `enum HeadKnown { Genesis, Known([u8;32]) }`
- `enum HeadMaybe { Genesis, Known([u8;32]), Unknown }`
- `Watermark<K, H>` where H is `HeadKnown` or `HeadMaybe`.
- Functions that require known heads (durable/applied in repl/gap buffer) only accept `Watermark<_, HeadKnown>`.

Option B (newtypes):
- `struct DurableHead(HeadStatus)` with `TryFrom<HeadStatus>` enforcing `Genesis|Known`.
- `Watermark<K>` uses `HeadKnown` for durable/applied; only snapshot inputs use `HeadMaybe` and must be validated to `HeadKnown` before entering session state.

Migration:
- Update watermark parsing and session snapshot ingestion to return `Result<Watermark<_, HeadKnown>, WatermarkError>` instead of allowing Unknown.
- Remove `unreachable!` branches; they should become impossible to compile.
- CLI/admin rendering can still show Unknown by using a distinct “maybe head” type if needed.

This follows “types should make time/causality explicit” and “the controversial type is probably the right type.”
title: Encode known/unknown head state in Watermark types

description:
**Problem**
Consistent visual styling across all commands requires a shared UI module with status icons, Ayu color palette, and rendering helpers.

**Design**
Create `src/cli/ui.rs` module with:

**Status Icons** (small Unicode, NOT emoji blobs):
- `○` open (neutral, hollow circle)
- `◐` in_progress (yellow, half-filled)
- `●` blocked (red, filled circle)
- `✓` closed (muted, checkmark)
- `❄` deferred (muted, snowflake)
- `📌` pinned (purple, pushpin)

**Ayu Theme Colors** (adaptive light/dark):
```rust
// Priority colors
P0: #f07171 (red, bold)
P1: #ff8f40 (orange)
P2: #e6b450 (gold)
P3/P4: neutral (no color)

// Status colors
in_progress: #ffb454 (yellow)
blocked: #f26d78 (red)
closed: #8090a0 (muted)
pinned: #d2a6ff (purple)

// Type colors
bug: #f26d78 (red)
epic: #d2a6ff (purple)
others: neutral
```

**Rendering Helpers**:
- `render_status_icon(status)` → colored icon
- `render_priority(pri)` → colored "● P0"
- `render_type(type)` → colored type badge
- `render_issue_compact(issue)` → one-line format
- `render_muted(text)` → gray text

**Design Principle**: Only actionable states get color. Open/closed use neutral colors; in_progress/blocked demand attention.

Reference: `/Users/darin/Projects/beads-rs-macstudio/tmp/beads/internal/ui/styles.go`

**Design Notes**
- Use `owo-colors` or `yansi` for ANSI colors with RGB support
- Detect terminal capabilities (TrueColor vs 256 vs basic)
- Respect NO_COLOR env var and non-TTY output
- MutedStyle for inactive/closed items
- BoldStyle for emphasis (P0 priority)
- Tree characters: `⎿ ` (child), `└─ ` (last), `  ` (indent)

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Status icons defined (○ ◐ ● ✓ ❄ 📌)
- [ ] Ayu color palette (adaptive light/dark)
- [ ] Priority colors (P0 bold red, P1 orange, P2 gold)
- [ ] Status colors (in_progress yellow, blocked red, closed muted)
- [ ] Type colors (bug red, epic purple)
- [ ] Rendering helper functions
- [ ] Respects NO_COLOR and terminal capabilities
- [ ] MutedStyle for closed issues (entire line)

**Files:**
- `src/cli/ui.rs` (new - core styling module)
- `Cargo.toml` (add owo-colors or yansi)
title: Status icons and Ayu theme styling system
